Skip to contents
Zresidual 0.1.0
  • Vignettes
  • Functions
  • News

Z-residual Diagnostics for brms Poisson Regression

Tingxuan Wu and Longhai Li

Source: vignettes/articles/demo_brms_pois.qmd

1 Preparation: Data Simulation and Model Caching

This vignette demonstrates the three distinct ways you can use Zresidual to evaluate Bayesian models fit via brms:

  1. Passing a Custom User-Defined Function.
  2. Triggering the Simulation-Predictive Function.
  3. Utilizing the Built-in Analytic Predictive Function.

To illustrate these methods effectively, we first generate a consistent testing environment. We simulate 100 datasets (where the true log-rate follows a non-linear sine wave) and fit two models to each: a misspecified linear model and a correctly specified non-linear model.

Because MCMC sampling is computationally expensive, we execute and cache these 200 model fits to disk. In the subsequent sections, we will instantly load these identical models to demonstrate how the three Zresidual calling methods yield identical, reliable diagnostics.

Code
if (!dir.exists(model_dir)) {
  dir.create(model_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

n_sim <- 100
n_sample <- 200 
sim_data <- vector("list", n_sim)

# Loop through iterations, checking for existing files before simulating/fitting
for(i in 1:n_sim) {
  
  data_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_", i, ".rds")
  wrong_model_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_", i, ".rds")
  correct_model_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_", i, ".rds")
  
  # --- 1. Check and Handle Data ---
  if (!force_refit && file.exists(data_file)) {
    sim_data[[i]] <- readRDS(data_file)
  } else {
    x <- runif(n_sample, 0, 10)
    y <- rpois(n_sample, exp(1.5 * sin(x)))
    
    dat_i <- data.frame(y = y, x = x)
    sim_data[[i]] <- dat_i
    saveRDS(dat_i, file = data_file)
  }
  
  # --- 2. Check and Fit Wrong Model ---
  if (force_refit || !file.exists(wrong_model_file)) {
    brm(
      y ~ x, 
      family = poisson(), 
      data = sim_data[[i]],
      backend = "cmdstanr",
      normalize = FALSE,
      file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_", i), 
      refresh = 0,
      cores = 4
    )
  }
  
  # --- 3. Check and Fit Correct Model ---
  if (force_refit || !file.exists(correct_model_file)) {
    brm(
      y ~ sin(x), 
      family = poisson(), 
      data = sim_data[[i]],
      backend = "cmdstanr",
      normalize = FALSE,
      file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_", i), 
      refresh = 0,
      cores = 4
    )
  }
}

2 Zresidual Using a Custom User-Defined log_pointpred Function

Zresidual is built with a highly extensible architecture. If your specific model family or backend is not natively supported yet, or if you simply want explicit control over the math, you can bypass the internal defaults entirely by passing a custom function to the log_pointpred argument.

Below, we define a custom analytical function that leverages brms::posterior_epred() to manually extract the expected rates (\lambda) and push them through R’s native distribution functions.

2.1 Defining a Custom Predictive Function

Code
log_pointpred_brms_pois <- function(fit, data = NULL, ...) {
  
  # 1. Resolve data
  data_in <- if (is.null(data)) fit$data else data
  
  # 2. Extract observed response safely
  model.data <- model.frame(fit$formula, data = data_in)
  model.var  <- names(model.data)
  
  response <- fit$formula$resp
  if (is.null(response) || !response %in% model.var) {
    response <- all.vars(fit$formula$formula)[1]
  }
  
  y_obs <- as.numeric(model.data[[response]])
  n <- length(y_obs)
  
  # 3. Extract Posterior Expected Values (lambda)
  lambda <- brms::posterior_epred(fit, dpar = "mu", newdata = data_in, ...)
  mc_used <- nrow(lambda)
  
  # 4. Calculate Log-PMF and Log-Survival Functions
  lpmf <- matrix(NA_real_, mc_used, n)
  lsf  <- matrix(NA_real_, mc_used, n)
  
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    lpmf[, i] <- dpois(y_obs[i], lambda = lambda[, i], log = TRUE)
    lsf[, i]  <- ppois(y_obs[i], lambda = lambda[, i],
                           lower.tail = FALSE, log.p = TRUE)
  }
  
  list(
    log_like = lpmf,
    log_surv  = lsf,
    is_discrete = rep(1L, n)
  )
}

2.2 Single Dataset Evaluation

To evaluate our model, we explicitly map the custom function to the log_pointpred argument. Zresidual handles the rest of the randomization, prediction extraction, and covariate binding automatically.

Code
set.seed(123) ## for reproducibility
Code
# Load the first cached dataset and model pair
dat <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_1.rds"))

fit_wrong <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_1"))
fit_correct <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_1"))

# Call Zresidual explicitly using the custom function
z_wrong   <- Zresidual(fit_wrong, data = dat, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10, log_pointpred = log_pointpred_brms_pois)
z_correct <- Zresidual(fit_correct, data = dat, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10, log_pointpred = log_pointpred_brms_pois)

2.3 Diagnostics with Z-residuals

Code
i <- 1 
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

qqnorm(z_wrong, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid Q-Q")
Outlier Indices : 124
Code
plot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs X")
Outlier Indices : 124
Code
boxplot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs LP")

qqnorm(z_correct, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid Q-Q")
plot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs X")
boxplot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs LP")

Replicated p-values of the same fitted models

Code
res_table <- data.frame(
  "Replicate" = paste("Rep", 1:ncol(z_wrong)),
  "SW_W"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_wrong)),
  "AOV_W"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "BL_W"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "SW_C"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_correct)),
  "AOV_C"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp")),
  "BL_C"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp"))
)

res_table %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4, 
    col.names = c("Replicate", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett"),
    caption = "Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates",
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center"
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Wrong Model (Linear)" = 3, "Correct Model (Non-linear)" = 3))
Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates
Wrong Model (Linear)
Correct Model (Non-linear)
Replicate SW AOV Bartlett SW AOV Bartlett
Rep 1 0.0008 0 0.0041 0.6813 0.2215 0.6805
Rep 2 0.0004 0 0.0025 0.7178 0.2476 0.7378
Rep 3 0.0009 0 0.0074 0.2816 0.4089 0.3039
Rep 4 0.0005 0 0.0005 0.4977 0.3158 0.5804
Rep 5 0.0003 0 0.0052 0.1013 0.2629 0.7599
Rep 6 0.0001 0 0.0001 0.4292 0.4229 0.7053
Rep 7 0.0021 0 0.0055 0.8052 0.2069 0.6760
Rep 8 0.0003 0 0.0001 0.4270 0.2392 0.7125
Rep 9 0.0007 0 0.0135 0.5594 0.3439 0.9062
Rep 10 0.0070 0 0.0694 0.2452 0.1368 0.7669

2.4 Checking the Sampling Distribution of ANOVA Nonlinearity Tests p-values

By batching this custom Zresidual call across our 100 simulated datasets, we construct a sampling distribution. The uniform p-values for the correctly specified model confirm that providing a user-defined function seamlessly integrates with the Zresidual statistical pipeline.

Code
user_res_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/user_pvalues.rds")

if (!force_recalcz && file.exists(user_res_file)) {
  res <- readRDS(user_res_file)
  p_c <- res$p_c
  p_w <- res$p_w
} else {
  p_c <- numeric(n_sim)
  p_w <- numeric(n_sim)
  
  for(i in 1:n_sim) {
    dat_sim <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_", i, ".rds"))
    fit_w_sim <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_", i), refresh = 0)
    z_w_sim <- Zresidual(fit_w_sim, data = dat_sim, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1, log_pointpred = log_pointpred_brms_pois)
    p_w[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_w_sim, X = "lp"))
    
    fit_c_sim <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_", i), refresh = 0)
    z_c_sim <- Zresidual(fit_c_sim, data = dat_sim, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1, log_pointpred = log_pointpred_brms_pois)
    p_c[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_c_sim, X = "lp"))
  }
  saveRDS(list(p_c = p_c, p_w = p_w), user_res_file)
}
Code
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(p_c, main = "Correct: Model P-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "lightblue", breaks = 10)
abline(h = n_sim / 10, col = "red", lty = 2)

hist(p_w, main = "Wrong: Small p-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "salmon", breaks = 10)

3 Zresidual Using the Simulation-Predictive Method

When an exact analytic mathematical formula is either unknown or computationally prohibitive for a specific model family, Zresidual provides a robust simulation mechanism: the Simulation-Predictive method.

By passing pred_method = "simulation", Zresidual automatically invokes the model’s native simulate or posterior_predict functions to draw empirical bounds, bypassing the need for explicit PDF/CDF math entirely.

3.1 Single Dataset Evaluation

Notice how we drop the log_pointpred argument here and explicitly request the "simulation" method. Zresidual automatically handles the translation from empirical draws to normalized residuals.

Code
set.seed(123) ## for reproducibility
Code
dat <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_1.rds"))
fit_wrong <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_1"))
fit_correct <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_1"))

# Trigger the Simulation Fallback method
z_wrong   <- Zresidual(fit_wrong, data = dat, pred_method = "simulation", mcmc_summarize = "iscv", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10)
z_correct <- Zresidual(fit_correct, data = dat, pred_method = "simulation", mcmc_summarize = "iscv", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10)

3.2 Diagnostics with Z-residuals

Code
i <- 1 
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

qqnorm(z_wrong, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid Q-Q")
Outlier Indices : 117, 190
Code
plot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs X")
Outlier Indices : 117, 190
Code
boxplot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs LP")

qqnorm(z_correct, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid Q-Q")
Outlier Indices : 29
Code
plot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs X")
Outlier Indices : 29
Code
boxplot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs LP")

Replicated p-values of the same fitted models

Code
res_table <- data.frame(
  "Replicate" = paste("Rep", 1:ncol(z_wrong)),
  "SW_W"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_wrong)),
  "AOV_W"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "BL_W"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "SW_C"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_correct)),
  "AOV_C"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp")),
  "BL_C"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp"))
)

res_table %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4, 
    col.names = c("Replicate", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett"),
    caption = "Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates",
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center"
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Wrong Model (Linear)" = 3, "Correct Model (Non-linear)" = 3))
Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates
Wrong Model (Linear)
Correct Model (Non-linear)
Replicate SW AOV Bartlett SW AOV Bartlett
Rep 1 0.0000 0 0.0043 0.0000 0.2492 0.0106
Rep 2 0.0000 0 0.0000 0.6252 0.3113 0.9179
Rep 3 0.0001 0 0.0007 0.8323 0.0659 0.7196
Rep 4 0.0000 0 0.0113 0.0000 0.3487 0.0048
Rep 5 0.0000 0 0.1333 0.4490 0.1456 0.5245
Rep 6 0.0001 0 0.0020 0.4056 0.1822 0.3657
Rep 7 0.0006 0 0.0136 0.0000 0.2291 0.0149
Rep 8 0.0000 0 0.0100 0.0000 0.4171 0.0273
Rep 9 0.0031 0 0.0510 0.0000 0.6220 0.0013
Rep 10 0.0000 0 0.4628 0.6198 0.2781 0.4636

3.3 Checking the Sampling Distribution of ANOVA Nonlinearity Tests p-values

Batching this approach confirms that the simulation fallback is statistically valid. It generates the identical expected behaviors (uniform distributions for correct models, skewed distributions for structural misspecifications) without requiring a single line of custom analytical code.

Code
sim_res_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/sim_pvalues.rds")

if (!force_recalcz && file.exists(sim_res_file)) {
  res <- readRDS(sim_res_file)
  p_c <- res$p_c
  p_w <- res$p_w
} else {
  p_c <- numeric(n_sim)
  p_w <- numeric(n_sim)

  for(i in 1:n_sim) {
    dat_sim <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_", i, ".rds"))
    
    fit_w_sim <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_", i))
    z_w_sim <- Zresidual(fit_w_sim, data = dat_sim, pred_method = "simulation", mcmc_summarize = "iscv", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1)
    p_w[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_w_sim, X = "lp"))
    
    fit_c_sim <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_", i))
    z_c_sim <- Zresidual(fit_c_sim, data = dat_sim, pred_method = "simulation", mcmc_summarize = "iscv", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1)
    p_c[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_c_sim, X = "lp"))
  }
  saveRDS(list(p_c = p_c, p_w = p_w), sim_res_file)
}
Code
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(p_c, main = "Correct: Model P-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "lightblue", breaks = 10)
abline(h = n_sim / 10, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

hist(p_w, main = "Wrong: Misspecification P-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "salmon", breaks = 10)

4 Zresidual Using the Built-In Analytical Routing

For standard model families evaluated on fully supported backends (like brms and standard glm), Zresidual aims to require zero configuration.

By simply omitting the predictive arguments entirely, Zresidual automatically queries the model object’s class and family string. It then silently routes the computation to highly optimized, natively written routines embedded directly within the package itself.

4.1 Single Dataset Evaluation

The simplest—and generally fastest—way to use Zresidual is to provide only the fitted model object and the data.

Code
set.seed(123) ## for reproducibility
Code
dat <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_1.rds"))
fit_wrong <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_1"))
fit_correct <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_1"))

# Calculate Z-residuals using default native routing
z_wrong   <- Zresidual(fit_wrong, data = dat, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10)
z_correct <- Zresidual(fit_correct, data = dat, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 10)

4.2 Diagnostics with Z-residuals

Code
i <- 1 
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

qqnorm(z_wrong, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid Q-Q")
Outlier Indices : 124
Code
plot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs X")
Outlier Indices : 124
Code
boxplot(z_wrong, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Wrong: Z-Resid vs LP")

qqnorm(z_correct, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid Q-Q")
plot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "x", category = dat$y, irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs X")
boxplot(z_correct, x_axis_var = "lp", irep = i, main = "Correct: Z-Resid vs LP")

Replicated p-values of the same fitted models

Code
res_table <- data.frame(
  "Replicate" = paste("Rep", 1:ncol(z_wrong)),
  "SW_W"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_wrong)),
  "AOV_W"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "BL_W"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_wrong, X = "lp")),
  "SW_C"      = as.numeric(sw.test.zresid(z_correct)),
  "AOV_C"     = as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp")),
  "BL_C"      = as.numeric(bartlett.test.zresid(z_correct, X = "lp"))
)

res_table %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4, 
    col.names = c("Replicate", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett", "SW", "AOV", "Bartlett"),
    caption = "Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates",
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center"
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Wrong Model (Linear)" = 3, "Correct Model (Non-linear)" = 3))
Diagnostic Test p-values Across Randomized Replicates
Wrong Model (Linear)
Correct Model (Non-linear)
Replicate SW AOV Bartlett SW AOV Bartlett
Rep 1 0.0008 0 0.0041 0.6813 0.2215 0.6805
Rep 2 0.0004 0 0.0025 0.7178 0.2476 0.7378
Rep 3 0.0009 0 0.0074 0.2816 0.4089 0.3039
Rep 4 0.0005 0 0.0005 0.4977 0.3158 0.5804
Rep 5 0.0003 0 0.0052 0.1013 0.2629 0.7599
Rep 6 0.0001 0 0.0001 0.4292 0.4229 0.7053
Rep 7 0.0021 0 0.0055 0.8052 0.2069 0.6760
Rep 8 0.0003 0 0.0001 0.4270 0.2392 0.7125
Rep 9 0.0007 0 0.0135 0.5594 0.3439 0.9062
Rep 10 0.0070 0 0.0694 0.2452 0.1368 0.7669

4.3 Checking the Sampling Distribution of ANOVA Nonlinearity Tests p-values

Our final replication confirms that the native built-in routing produces the exact same robust diagnostic sampling distributions as our custom extraction logic, achieving uniform p-values for properly specified models.

Code
ana_res_file <- paste0(model_dir, "/ana_pvalues.rds")

if (!force_recalcz && file.exists(ana_res_file)) {
  res <- readRDS(ana_res_file)
  p_c <- res$p_c
  p_w <- res$p_w
} else {
  p_c <- numeric(n_sim)
  p_w <- numeric(n_sim)
  
  for(i in 1:n_sim) {
    dat_sim <- readRDS(paste0(model_dir, "/data_sim_", i, ".rds"))
    
    fit_w_sim <- brm(y ~ x, family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/wrong_sim_", i), refresh = 0)
    z_w_sim <- Zresidual(fit_w_sim, data = dat_sim, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1)
    p_w[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_w_sim, X = "lp"))
    
    fit_c_sim <- brm(y ~ sin(x), family = poisson(), data = dat_sim, backend = "cmdstanr", file = paste0(model_dir, "/correct_sim_", i), refresh = 0)
    z_c_sim <- Zresidual(fit_c_sim, data = dat_sim, mcmc_summarize = "post", randomized = TRUE, nrep = 1)
    p_c[i] <- as.numeric(aov.test.zresid(z_c_sim, X = "lp"))
  }
  
  saveRDS(list(p_c = p_c, p_w = p_w), ana_res_file)
}
Code
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(p_c, main = "Correct: Model P-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "lightblue", breaks = 10)
abline(h = n_sim / 10, col = "red", lty = 2)

hist(p_w, main = "Wrong: Small p-values", xlab = "p-value", col = "salmon", breaks = 10)

On this page
